exemplar.title: How do five concentrations of pineapple (Ananas comosus) juice (20%, 40%, 60%, 80%, 100%) inhibit the enzymatic browning of a 5 ml pear (Pyrus Communis) juice sample in 15 minutes as measured by 500 nm blue absorbance values?

detailedExemplarData.title: How do five concentrations of pineapple (Ananas comosus) juice (20%, 40%, 60%, 80%, 100%) inhibit the enzymatic browning of a 5 ml pear (Pyrus Communis) juice sample in 15 minutes as measured by 500 nm blue absorbance values?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Provides a specific, wellâdeveloped context linking enzymatic browning chemistry to realâworld applications
Clear explanation of methodological considerations with detailed control rationale and preliminary tests
Procedure description includes apparatus lists, dilution tables and calibration steps that allow reproducibility
weaknesses: Economic and industrial significance is mentioned but not explored in depth
Justification for choosing five concentrations and the 15 min reaction time is not provided
Minor ambiguities in procedure (e.g. mixed references to pipettes vs measuring cylinders and an imprecise cuvette volume)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand scores of 5 (context), 5 (methodological considerations) and 4 (methodology description) place the work solidly in the high band. The context is specific and appropriate, dataâcollection considerations are well explained, and the procedure is reproducible with only minor omissions. A few small gaps prevent the maximum mark, so 5/6 is awarded.

strengths: Data tables include all trials, means and standard deviations; graphical presentation is coherent with error bars
Appropriate linear regression analysis with RÂ² and Pearson r clearly explained
Processed data are accurate and relevant to the research question
weaknesses: Dependent-variable units are mislabeled as ânmâ instead of absorbance units
No uncertainty propagation into concentration values and missing cuvette uncertainty
Some summation values in tables appear inconsistent and axes are inverted in graphs
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Recording and processing of data are clear, with appropriate analysis, but typographical errors, omitted uncertainty calculations and minor inaccuracies (axis inversion, unit mislabelling) mean the work fits the 3â4 band at a high level. Thus 4/6 is appropriate.

strengths: Conclusion restates the strong negative correlation and statistical significance clearly
Discussion of reliability considers small SDs and equipment precision in context
Relevant comparison to peerâreviewed literature with mechanistic linkage to accepted biochemical theory
weaknesses: Comparison is limited to a single external study; broader literature context would strengthen the argument
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is fully consistent with the analysis, justified and placed in the accepted scientific context, meeting topâband descriptors. Slight overstatement on uncertainties and limited breadth of literature keep it at 5/6.

strengths: Identifies specific methodological weaknesses and explains their relative impact on data variability
Proposes realistic, targeted improvements linked directly to each identified limitation
Outlines meaningful further investigations to build on the study
weaknesses: Does not quantify anticipated error reduction from suggested improvements
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation explains the impact of specific weaknesses and proposes relevant improvements, satisfying the topâband descriptor. The lack of quantitative estimation of error reduction prevents a perfect score, so 5/6 is awarded.

strandSelections.reasoning: The conclusion directly restates the observed strong negative correlation, cites statistical significance and discusses reliability in light of small SDs and equipment precision.  It is fully consistent with the processed data.  Minor over-statement about neglecting uncertainties keeps the award to 5 rather than 6, but the work clearly meets top-band criteria.

Raw-data tables include all concentration levels, five trials and appropriate units; processed tables give means and SDs.  Graphical presentation is coherent and axis labels are provided (though axes are inverted).  Some typographic clutter (âValue 1â¦Value 30â) slightly hinders clarity, but overall the recording/processing is **clear** even if not perfectly formatted, satisfying the 3â4 descriptor.

Means, SDs, a Pearson r, RÂ² and a correctly chosen linear regression constitute appropriate processing for the research question.  Calculations are accurate and explained.  Minor errors (absorbance labelled ânmâ, inverted axes, unused uncertainty values) represent âsome significant inaccuraciesâ but do not undermine the overall appropriateness, so a high 3â4 band mark is warranted.

Three concrete weaknesses are identified (PPO variability, elapsed time before mixing, colour interference) and each is accompanied by a reasoned discussion of how it could skew results (e.g., higher SDs at 0 % and 100 %).  The student clearly articulates **relative impact** (âremains a significant methodological limitationâ), satisfying the explanatory requirement of the 5â6 band, though limited quantitative estimation of error impact warrants a 5 rather than 6.

The student compares findings with a peer-reviewed study on PPO inhibition in pears, noting both agreement and a point of divergence (ascorbic acid stability).  Additional mechanistic explanations (copper chelation, antioxidant action) connect results to accepted biochemical theory.  These relevant, explicit comparisons justify the conclusion; hence the strand belongs in the 5â6 band, with a slight deduction for limited breadth of literature cited.

The student frames the research question after a comprehensive introduction that discusses PPOâmediated browning, the chemistry of citric/ascorbic acids and food-industry relevance.  The background moves smoothly from general enzymology to the very specific scenario of using pineapple juice to inhibit browning in pear juice, so the reader sees exactly **why** the stated question matters and how it fits real-world practice.  This represents a clear, specific and appropriate context rather than a broad or generic one, justifying placement in the 5â6 band; a full 6 is not awarded because the economic significance to industry, although mentioned, is not explored in depth.

Instrument uncertainties are listed and briefly discussed (âlikely pose no significant impactâ), and SDs are calculated and shown as error bars.  However, no uncertainty is attached to concentration values, no propagation is attempted and units for absorbance are incorrectly shown as nm.  Thus uncertainties are considered but with significant omissions, matching the 3â4 band; the weaker treatment justifies a score at the lower end of that band.

For each stated weakness a feasible, targeted improvement is proposed (pooling pears, colder environment, alternative juices).  The suggestions are linked explicitly to how they would mitigate the identified errors, demonstrating explanatory depth.  A slightly fuller justification of the practicality/cost of implementing these changes would be needed for the absolute maximum, so a 5 within the top band is appropriate.

A detailed variables table, rationale for each control, and explicit mention of settling pineapple precipitate and uncontrolled temperature show that the student **explains** how reliable, sufficient data will be obtained.  The effect of coloured juice on absorbance and of PPO variability between pears are anticipated, demonstrating understanding beyond a simple list.  Minor gaps (e.g. no justification of five concentrations or 15-min reaction time) prevent the maximum mark, but the strand comfortably meets the top-band descriptor.

The step-by-step procedure, dilution table, apparatus list and calibration details would allow another investigator to reproduce the work; however, there are a few ambiguities: conflicting references to pipettes *and* measuring cylinders for the same transfer, unclear cuvette volume (stated âfill up to 80 %â without absolute volume) and uncertain quantities for some apparatus in Table 2.  These small omissions align with the 3â4 band rather than the flawless description required for 5â6.

annotations.comment: The student provides a thorough mechanistic explanation of enzymatic browning, outlining the PPO-catalyzed oxidation steps clearly and setting a solid biochemical foundation for the investigationâs context.
Economic and industrial relevance is mentioned in passing; the student should elaborate on specific commercial applications or costâbenefit considerations to deepen the contextâs specificity.
The research question is concise, targeted and situated in a specific context, making clear both the variables and the measurement method.

The rationale for choosing five concentration levels and a 15 min reaction time is not fully justified. The student should explain why these specific values were selected, perhaps based on preliminary data or literature.
The units for the dependent variable (blue absorbance) are incorrectly given as ânm.â These should be unitless absorbance values or arbitrary units; correct this to avoid confusion.
The controlled variables table is comprehensive and demonstrates clear planning to minimize experimental error by specifying how each variable is managed.
Room temperature fluctuations are noted but not controlled; consider monitoring temperature or conducting the experiment in a thermostatted environment to reduce kinetic variability.
Preliminary testing is effectively used to identify unsuitable pear varieties and colorimetric interferences, strengthening the methodological rationale.
The materials and apparatus table omits uncertainty for the cuvette; include this value or justify its exclusion to complete the uncertainty analysis.
The use of âââ symbols instead of numeric values for quantities in Table 2 is unclear. Replace symbolic notation with explicit numbers to improve reproducibility.
The choice of a linear trendline is justified, but the student could include a brief comparison to a polynomial fit to illustrate the rationale more fully.
Step 6 of the procedure refers to using both a pipette and a measuring cylinder for the same volume transfer; standardize a single technique to avoid ambiguity in replication.
The 30 min settling time is used without justification; reference your preliminary pH stability test or literature to confirm this equilibration period.
Qualitative observations table captures extremes but omits intermediate concentrations; consider timing or colorimetric thresholds to quantify qualitative data.
Processed data table effectively reports means and SDs, but does not incorporate instrument uncertainty into the error analysis; add propagation or comment on its negligible effect.
Graph 1 is described as showing absorbance in ânm,â which is incorrect; update the axis label to âabsorbance unitsâ or similar.
The explanation of RÂ² in the context of model fit is clear and accurately communicated, reinforcing the strength of the linear correlation.
The Pearson correlation formula is correctly presented in LaTeX and matches standard notation, facilitating reproducibility.
Values in Table 9 (e.g., \(\Sigma X^2=2.00\)) appear inconsistent with raw data; double-check these summations or clarify any rounding.
The significance table (Table 11) is included but could be augmented by explicitly stating the chosen significance level (e.g., 0.05) upfront.
The conclusion succinctly restates the alternative hypothesis and aligns fully with the quantitative and qualitative findings.
The comparison to a single peer-reviewed study is relevant but could be broadened by citing additional sources to strengthen the scientific context comparison.
The evaluation table identifies specific methodological limitations and explains their relative impact on data variability, meeting top-band expectations.
Improvement suggestions are realistic and linked to identified weaknesses, but the student should quantify anticipated error reduction where possible.
The further investigation section outlines meaningful next steps and suggests focused experimental comparisons, providing a strong basis for future work.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The research question is stated without context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting data relevant to the research question are stated.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data lacks the detail to allow for the investigation to be reproduced.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced with few ambiguities or omissions.
The research question is outlined within a broad context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are described.
The research question is described within a specific and appropriate context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are explained.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The recording and processing of the data is communicated but is neither clear nor precise.
Some processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with major omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.
The recording and processing of data shows limited evidence of the consideration of uncertainties.
The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with some significant omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.
The communication of the recording and processing of the data is either clear or precise.
The recording and processing of data shows evidence of a consideration of uncertainties but with some significant omissions or inaccuracies.
The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out appropriately and accurately.
The recording and processing of data shows evidence of an appropriate consideration of uncertainties.
The communication of the recording and processing of the data is both clear and precise.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Communication of data recording and processing

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The conclusion makes superficial comparison to the accepted scientific context.
A conclusion is stated that is relevant to the research question but is not supported by the analysis presented.
A conclusion is described that makes some relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.
A conclusion is described that is relevant to the research question but is not fully consistent with the analysis presented.
A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.
A conclusion is justified through relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
Realistic improvements to the investigation are stated.
The report states generic methodological weaknesses or limitations.
Realistic improvements to the investigation that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are described.
The report describes specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.
Realistic improvements to the investigation, that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are explained.
The report explains the relative impact of specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.
